safety_boundaries: Do not modify OnionQuant runtime, Cron, context_state, Inbox, or Outbox.
Public repository contains only generic code and fully synthetic data.
Do not publish real company documents, endpoints, model names, certificates, credentials, indexes, logs, screenshots, or evaluation questions.
No force-push, destructive Git reset, AI Cron, polling worker, arbitrary Shell/Python/Web/MCP tool, model-generated SQL, or physical equipment control.

chairman_corrections.summary: Search current Web and GitHub evidence before architecture, implementation, or bug-fix design; prefer mature deployable components over hand-written substitutes.
Use bounded parallel Agents for independent research and review while keeping one implementation write owner and preserving the project hard concurrency cap.

Treat commercial HVAC as the first deep domain: unit configuration, controls, field changes, meeting records, decisions, SOPs, alarms, telemetry, and background knowledge must drive repeated evaluation and optimization.
Before release, perform a complete human HVAC-engineer UX journey and review every button, label, placement, feedback state, error message, citation view, destructive action, and mobile flow.
Push the finished release to GitHub and provide a detailed zero-context company-PC handoff that a less capable Agent can execute safely end to end.
Persist requirements, decisions, task state, failures, evidence, and next actions in a durable ledger/checkpoint structure rather than relying on conversational history.

Support natural-language review of commercial-HVAC defrost logic over user-selected ten-second telemetry windows by combining exact control documents, deterministic temporal replay, bounded AI orchestration, and explicit uncertainty.
